content.problemStatement:
For a given array B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​ of length at least 333, let's define its **weight** as the largest value of (Bi−Bj)⋅(Bj−Bk)(B_i-B_j)\cdot(B_j-B_k)(Bi​−Bj​)⋅(Bj​−Bk​) over all possible triples (i,j,k)(i, j, k)(i,j,k) with 1≤i,j,k≤M1 \le i, j, k \le M1≤i,j,k≤M and i≠j,j≠k,k≠ii \neq j, j \neq k, k \neq ii=j,j=k,k=i.

You are given a sorted array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ (that is, A1≤A2≤…≤ANA_1 \le A_2 \le \ldots \le A_NA1​≤A2​≤…≤AN​).

Calculate the sum of weights of all contiguous subarrays of AAA of length at least 333. That is, count the sum of weights of arrays [Ai,Ai+1,…,Aj][A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_j][Ai​,Ai+1​,…,Aj​] over all 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N with j−i≥2j-i\ge 2j−i≥2.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the sum of weights of all subarrays of AAA of length at least 333.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 6000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N (3 ≤ N ≤ 3000). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN in non‑decreasing order (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^6).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the sum of the weights of all contiguous subarrays of length at least 3.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first array the subarrays of length ≥3 are [1,2,3], [2,3,4] and [1,2,3,4]. Their weights are 1, 1 and 2 respectively, sum = 4. For the second array the algorithm evaluates all 7 subarrays of length ≥3 and the total weight equals 1041808.
Only one subarray of length 3 exists. All elements are equal, so (Bi‑Bj)*(Bj‑Bk)=0 for any triple, weight = 0.

solution.approach: For a sorted subarray B[l…r] (l < r, r‑l+1 ≥ 3) the weight is max_{j∈(l,r)} (B[j]-B[l])·(B[r]-B[j]). With fixed endpoints the expression is a concave quadratic in B[j] and attains its maximum when B[j] is closest to the midpoint (B[l]+B[r])/2. While expanding the right end r for a fixed left l, the optimal j moves only to the right, therefore a two‑pointer scan can find the best j for every r in amortised O(1). Algorithm: for each l from 0 to N‑3 set j = l+1; for r from l+2 to N‑1 while j+1 < r and (A[j+1]-A[l])*(A[r]-A[j+1]) ≥ (A[j]-A[l])*(A[r]-A[j]) increase j; add (A[j]-A[l])*(A[r]-A[j]) to the answer. This runs in O(N^2) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case (worst‑case about 9·10^6 operations for N=3000).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        A = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        total = 0
        for l in range(N - 2):
            j = l + 1
            Al = A[l]
            for r in range(l + 2, N):
                Ar = A[r]
                while j + 1 < r:
                    # compare (A[j+1]-Al)*(Ar-A[j+1]) with (A[j]-Al)*(Ar-A[j])
                    left1 = A[j] - Al
                    right1 = Ar - A[j]
                    left2 = A[j + 1] - Al
                    right2 = Ar - A[j + 1]
                    if left2 * right2 >= left1 * right1:
                        j += 1
                    else:
                        break
                total += (A[j] - Al) * (Ar - A[j])
        out_lines.append(str(total))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
